data.title: Germany's unemployment rate steady at 6.1% in December
data.description:
Germany's seasonally adjusted unemployment rate remained at 6.1% in December 2024, slightly below market expectations of 6.2% but still near its highest level since February 2021.

The number of unemployed persons increased to 2869K in December from 2860K in November.

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR) (DAX:IND)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Tesla China sales hit record high level in December
data.description:
Though Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) global deliveries slipped 1.1% in 2024, sales in China mustered up despite ongoing price wars and stiff competition from homegrown brands like BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF), NIO (NIO), and others.

Tesla (TSLA) car sales in China were up 12.8% month-over-month in December to a record high of 83,000 units, Reuters reported, citing Tesla China.

Overall in 2024, Tesla (TSLA) sold 657,000 units, up 8% year-over-year, and deliveries for the final quarter of 2024 were up 2.28% from 484,507. About 196,900 vehicles were sold in China in Q4, according to calculations CnEVPost.

The EV maker delivered 36.7% of its cars in the year 2024 to customers in China, its second-largest market, based on the sales figures.

Tesla (TSLA) extended discounts and zero-interest financing on some of its cars as the EV price war in China enters a third year. The U.S. EV giant (TSLA) also had to cut its sales team in China due to stiff competition and tepid demand.

data.title: Roadzen prices capital raise of $5M via stock offering
data.description:
• Roadzen (NASDAQ:RDZN) has announced the pricing of 2,222,300 ordinary shares at a public offering price of $2.25 per share, for gross proceeds of approximately $5 million.
  • All the ordinary shares are being offered by the company.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 6, 2025.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for sales, marketing, research, development, working capital, and general corporate purposes, including personnel and capital expenditures, and may also use a portion of the net proceeds to repay indebtedness outstanding.

data.title: Himax collaborates with Calumino for AI thermal sensing and imaging
data.description:
Himax Technologies (NASDAQ:HIMX) and Calumino, an intelligent thermal imaging solutions company announced their strategic partnership and unveiled their innovative, CMOS imager-based optical thermal sensor.

The partnership marks a significant step forward in AI thermal sensing and imaging across industries.

The strategic partnership betweenteh two companies will also prioritize innovative CMOS imager-based optical thermal sensor advancement and scalable manufacturing process development to deliver high-quality solutions at competitive costs

“Our collaboration with Calumino underscores our commitment to driving innovation and delivering value to our customers. The CES product samples provide a glimpse into what the future holds for this exciting technology.” said Jordan Wu, President and CEO of Himax.

data.title: MINISO joins hands with China's major gaming title Black Myth: Wukong
data.description:
Shares of MINISO Group (NYSE:MNSO) are poised for a weekly increase of more than 6% on the Hong Kong stock exchange after it partnered with the successful video game "Black Myth: Wukong."

The collaboration designates MINISO as an official partner, with plans to launch co-branded products in 2025.

Black Myth: Wukong—debuted in August 2024, is a notable AAA game and has achieved impressive sales figures, exceeding 23 million copies on the Steam platform.

The partnership aligns with MINISO's (MNSO) strategy to enhance consumer engagement through collaborations with popular intellectual properties.

This strategic move is expected to bolster MINISO's (MNSO) brand presence and attract a wider consumer base.

data.title: Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund reports unaudited NAV as of Dec 31, 2024
data.description:
• Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund's (NYSE:KYN) unaudited net asset value was $2.4 billion, or $13.90 per share, as of December 31.

  • Its asset coverage ratio with respect to senior securities representing indebtedness was 653%, and its coverage ratio for debt and preferred stock was 488%.

data.description:

data.title: Algoma Steel sees Q4 adjusted EBITDA -C$55M to -C$65M; total steel shipments of 545K-550K tons
data.description:
• Algoma Steel (NASDAQ:ASTL) -2.78% in Friday's premarket trading after issuing guidance for FQ4 ending December 31 total steel shipments of 545K-550K tons and adjusted EBITDA of negative C$55M to negative C$65M.

  • "Despite market headwinds, the company reported solid operational execution, particularly in its plate business. Despite macroeconomic uncertainties, Algoma is poised to capitalize on improving fundamentals," said Michael Garcia, CEO.

  • "The company's Electric Arc Furnace project reached a significant milestone with the start of cold commissioning activities. While record snowfall in late November and early December briefly impacted project work, management doesn't expect material delays in achieving first steel production by the end of Q1 2025. The company continues its strategic transformation toward becoming a leading low-carbon steel producer in North America," he noted.

data.title: Asia-Pacific markets trade mixed tracking fall on Wall Street, PBOC reportedly signals rate cuts
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mixed on Friday after a lackluster start to the year on Wall Street. Market participants also assess the comments from the People’s Bank of China, indicating that it would likely reduce interest rates from the current 1.5% "at an appropriate time" this year.

Traders are also closely monitoring geopolitical developments, including the potential impact of Donald Trump’s return to the White House.

Japan (NKY:IND) market closed.

China (SHCOMP) fell -1.63% and Shenzhen Component indexes traded near the flat line on Friday, stabilizing after a recent downturn, and the offshore yuan rose to around 7.32 per dollar, rebounding from three sessions of losses following a signal from the People’s Bank of China of a significant shift in its monetary policy approach to stimulate the Chinese economy.

The People’s Bank of China announced that it would likely reduce interest rates from the current 1.5% "at an appropriate time" this year, marking a significant shift in its monetary policy approach, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing comments the bank made to the newspaper.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +0.08%

India (SENSEX) fell -0.45% to 79,333 in morning trade on Friday, halting gains from the previous two sessions.

Investors continued to monitor pre-quarterly business updates for clues ahead of the upcoming earnings season.

Australia (AS51) rose +0.60% closing at 8,251 and extending its positive momentum from the previous session.

The benchmark KOSPI surged nearly 2% on Friday, climbing above 2,440 and recovering from three-week lows as investors brushed off the latest political turmoil in South Korea. The South Korean won strengthened past 1,466 per dollar, marking its second consecutive session of gains.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended in red in a volatile first trading session of the new year, extending the slump from late 2024 into January.

U.S. stock futures rose slightly on Friday after the major indexes extended losses in the first trading session of the new year: Dow +0.10%; S&P 500 +0.21%; Nasdaq +0.30%.

Looking ahead, investors are focused on Friday’s ISM Manufacturing Index and further comments from Federal Reserve officials for additional economic insights.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: People’s Bank of China signals interest rate reduction this year amid policy shift
data.description:
The People’s Bank of China announced that it would likely reduce interest rates from the current 1.5% "at an appropriate time" this year, marking a significant shift in its monetary policy approach, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing comments the bank made to the newspaper.

The remarks were consistent with the Chinese Politburo's December decision to shift the country's monetary policy stance from 'prudent' to 'appropriately loose', the first such shift in 14 years, aimed at bolstering the sluggish economy.

The central bank also noted a shift in focus toward "the role of interest rate adjustments" rather than "quantitative objectives" for loan growth, aligning its approach more closely with that of the US Federal Reserve and the European Central Bank.

This initiative is part of a broader interest rate reform program, which government advisors have described as an "arduous task.", as per the reports.

The Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indexes traded near the flat line on Friday, stabilizing after a recent downturn, and the offshore yuan rose to around 7.32 per dollar, rebounding from three sessions of losses following a signal from the People’s Bank of China of a significant shift in its monetary policy approach to stimulate the Chinese economy.

ETFs: (FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), (KURE).
